title: Eva Design Changes Between 10 and 20 Retcon or Not
display_title: Eva Design Changes Between 10 and 20 Retcon or Not
wikitext:
[]: Eva Design Changes Between 1.0 and 2.0: Retcon or Not?             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Dec 22, 2009 2:39 am NO SPOILERS IN THIS THREAD; ASPECTS OF 2.0 DISCUSSED ARE PURELY COSMETIC. To see an in-depth comparison of the differences between Unit 01 in 1.0 and 2.0, watch this YouTube video starting at around 5:30. Yes, it's the figurines being discussed, but the changes reflect canonical changes in the film (except for the arm text, which was never present in the films), which can be confirmed by viewing concept art. When first noting the differences in the Evas' armor months back, at first I simply assumed the redesigns to be a retcon, such as the differences in the ship between Alien and Aliens (or, more wankily, the contradictions Arthur C. Clarke deliberately placed in the books in his Space Odyssey series to imply they took place in different continuities). Reichu was quick to point out, however, that new armor could have been made for the Evas between the events of the two films. Oh yeah, I thought. The Evas are biological and their armor can be changed whenever necessary. But wait a minute, I just thought now, several months after the fact while Reichu is still nowhere to be seen even though she should be back around now. What would be the point of redoing the Evas' armor in such a manner? Sure, Unit 00 was heavily damaged during the fight against Ramiel, but in the final shot of Unit 01, the armor seemed completely intact. And considering the stretched budget, redoing so much of an Evangelion's armor for cosmetic purposes seems rather insane. So which is more likely, do you think, in your opinion? Retcon or expensive makeover?

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Tue Dec 22, 2009 3:35 am It's not that much of a big deal, thanks to minor changes they sell more toys.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Tue Dec 22, 2009 4:29 am Sort of a slight retcon in my opinion, similar to the EOE redesign of Unit-02. The differences are pretty minor but, like the figure reviewer mentioned, they give it a more menacing, scary feel, which goes along with the finale of 2.0. Maybe a retcon isn't the right phrase, more taking artistic license with the established design.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Tue Dec 22, 2009 6:18 am A visual narrative should never be set in concrete and things should be allowed to evolve. The cosmetic changes in 2.0 from 1.0 were nice. Simple and not distracting in the least. I just hope once this whole Rebuild affair is over they don't bring out an Eva 1.5 where the slight cosmetic changes from the sequel are digitally inserted into the first film.

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Tue Dec 22, 2009 6:59 am Changing the design in order to be able to sell another series of figures sounds pretty logical. At least 01 looked fine in 1.0/2.0, looks like unit 00 got hit with the ugly/fat stick :(

[]:             Quote Post by Rodeo » Tue Dec 22, 2009 9:47 am Things like these don't need an in-universe explanation. In fact, it's better when they don't have one. Yamashita wanted to make Eva-01 awesomer, that's all there is to it. I think he succeeded.

[]:             Quote Post by jayngfet » Tue Dec 22, 2009 7:02 pm toe mash wrote: Changing the design in order to be able to sell another series of figures sounds pretty logical. At least 01 looked fine in 1.0/2.0, looks like unit 00 got hit with the ugly/fat stick :(

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Tue Dec 22, 2009 11:26 pm People change their minds about their works over periods of time (I sure do), so it wouldn't surprise me that some guy had a new idea for how the Evas should look and just wanted to put it in there.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Dec 22, 2009 11:27 pm If these are retcons, I'm not saying they're a problem. In fact, with the Alien films in particular I like thinking of them as being in separate continuities, as every film in the Quadrilogy starts by destroying the accomplishments of the previous film. Anyway, the cosmetic differences are indeed minor from an entertainment standpoint, but if they are in-universe changes and not retcons, they'd certainly be a big deal for NERV. Finally, a bit of possible fanwankery: as I said, Arthur C. Clarke deliberately put contradictions in each of the books in his Space Odyssey series to imply they took place in different universes. If he hadn't outright said that was the case, there would be no evidence that these weren't just continuity errors, possibly deliberate and possibly not; since so many Clarkeian references (and 2001 film version references) abound in Eva and Anno is certainly familiar with his works, is it too radical to speculate that Rebuild might be doing the same thing? I'm not saying I think this is the case, of course, but it's an interesting thought.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Wed Dec 23, 2009 8:35 am NAveryW wrote: Finally, a bit of possible fanwankery: as I said, Arthur C. Clarke deliberately put contradictions in each of the books in his Space Odyssey series to imply they took place in different universes. If he hadn't outright said that was the case, there would be no evidence that these weren't just continuity errors, possibly deliberate and possibly not; since so many Clarkeian references (and 2001 film version references) abound in Eva and Anno is certainly familiar with his works, is it too radical to speculate that Rebuild might be doing the same thing? I'm not saying I think this is the case, of course, but it's an interesting thought.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Dec 23, 2009 4:15 pm master_lloyd wrote: Most of us aren't even sure yet whether Rebuild itself is a separate continuity from the original series

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Wed Dec 23, 2009 5:15 pm How?

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Wed Dec 23, 2009 5:26 pm The entirety of Rebuild actually takes place on the other end of Tokyo-3, simultaneously with the original series. Final is going to feature a glorious doppelganger showdown, preferably involving two Gendos duking it out from the backs of saddled GNRs. ...I can dream, can't I?

[]:             Quote Post by bladerj » Fri Dec 25, 2009 5:11 pm its not a retcon, its a toy manufacture change to better reflect the product it was inspired by. The first version looked more like the evas from the original series, they didnt reflect the rebuild version, in fact find pictures of the original series revoltech and the rebuild 1.0 version, with exception of the upper torso and colour change, its the same figure, wich makes sense, in a industrial point to use the same process to creat them.

[]:             Quote Post by jayngfet » Sun Dec 27, 2009 7:36 pm Maybe just maybe they're an upgrade of sort? Stronger and lightweight to allow better protection and movement? I can see it happening, since the EVAs have shown their worth and saved thousands of lives it's not unreasonable to assume they've gotten better funding.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Dec 27, 2009 8:50 pm jayngfet wrote: Maybe just maybe they're an upgrade of sort? Stronger and lightweight to allow better protection and movement? I can see it happening, since the EVAs have shown their worth and saved thousands of lives it's not unreasonable to assume they've gotten better funding.

[]:             Quote Post by bladerj » Sun Dec 27, 2009 9:25 pm There is no diference between the movie model 1.0 and the 2.0, only the toys are diferent, the only update that happened was eva 00 gaining shoulder fins.

[]:             Quote Post by Rodeo » Sun Dec 27, 2009 10:18 pm There are small differences. [URL=http://img690.imageshack.us/i/eva01progression.jpg/] [/URL]

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Sun Dec 27, 2009 11:08 pm Would be nice if Rebuild Unit-01 would STAND UP STRAIGHT.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Tue Dec 29, 2009 4:58 pm Fazmotron wrote: Would be nice if Rebuild Unit-01 would STAND UP STRAIGHT.
